revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:伊射奈岐神社 (天理市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Izanagi jinja.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.55744444444444|N|135.84566666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}|architecture_style={{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}}} {{infobox Shinto shrine | name = Izanagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Izanagi jinja.JPG|280px|]] | location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Yanagimoto Town 1899 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|26.8|N|135|50|44.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Izanagi Shrine (Tenri)}} | Deities = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} | ShrineRank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)・[[Village shrine]] | consecration_year = | MainBuilding = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Festival = [[October 15]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara Prefecture|label=Izanagi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Izanagi Shrine''' (伊射奈岐神社, ''Izanagi Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine is situated near the {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}) and {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the Izanagi Shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under {{ill|Kiijo District|ja|城上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiijo District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式上郡|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486985}}. Historically referred to as '''Yangimoto Tenjin''' or '''Yangimoto Tenmangu''', it is said to have been originally located southeast of its current site in Yamada Gaito. By the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|qid=Q8432|lt=Bunmei|1=Civilization}} era, the shrine had been relocated to its present location and became the {{ill|Soshagu|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soshagu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=Chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97479976}} (general protector) of Yangimoto.''Daijoin Temple Records'' (大乗院寺社雑事記), entry for Bunmei 17 (1485), 9th month, 26th day (Julian date: November 12). During the {{ill|Kanshō|en|Kanshō|qid=Q1143928|lt=Kansei|1=Kanshō}} period, on the 6th day of the 4th month (1461), {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7138674}}, the head priest of {{ill|Daijoin|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijoin|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16766994}}, visited the shrine. Similarly, on the 28th day of the 2nd month of {{ill|Tenmon|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon|qq=}} 22 (1553), {{ill|Sanjo Nishikimichitaka|ja|三条西公条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo Nishikimichitaka|1=Kinʼeda Sanjōnishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355850}} made a pilgrimage. Two years later, on the 29th day of the 8th month (1555), {{ill|Emperor Go-Nara|en|Emperor Go-Nara|qid=Q349467|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|1=Emperor Go-Nara}} issued an imperial decree changing the festival's date from August to September and tasked {{ill|Yanagihara Mitsukazu|ja|柳原淳光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagihara Mitsukazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with praying for the restoration of the imperial house. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine received support from the {{ill|Yanagimoto Domain|en|Yanagimoto Domain|qid=Q3034568|lt=Yanagimoto Domain|1=Yanagimoto Domain}} and was referred to as the domain shrine of the {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|qid=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan|1=Oda clan}}. Previously, the shrine's main ritual was held on the 10th day of the 8th month, during which three {{ill|to|ja|斗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/to|1=To (unit)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1078168}} of rice were offered as {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}} fees.''Sangain Kasho'' (三箇院家抄). Until the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the festival included unique offerings such as "monkey bundles," "fox bundles," and "pomegranate bundles," which were part of the deity's procession. Two wooden plaques, created during the 10th day of the 8th month in {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|qid=Q1145064|lt=Genki|1=Genki (era)}} 2 (1571), remain as artifacts. Made from cherry wood, these plaques depict items like sweetfish, plums, and shrimp on one side and carp on the other. == Access == * A 5-minute walk from {{ill|Yanagimoto Station|en|Yanagimoto Station|qid=Q4188827|lt=Yanagimoto Station|1=Yanagimoto Station}} on the {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|qid=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}} operated by {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|JR西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/West Japan Railway Company|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西日本旅客鉄道|1=West Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q502125}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|editor=|title=Japanese Place name Encyclopedia (Online Edition)|publisher=Shogakukan|year=|isbn=|ref=harv}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|伊射奈岐神社 (天理市)|version=87337478|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:伊射奈岐神社 (天理市)]] {{Commons category|Izanagi-jinja (Tenri)}} {{wikidata link|Q11379323}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamanobe-no-michi]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
